title: Enicostema occidentale
taxonomy: Enicostema occidentale is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. The genus Enicostema is a small one, consisting of a few species of flowering plants. Enicostema occidentale is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The classification of Enicostema occidentale also places it within the tribe Rubieae, which is a group of flowering plants characterized by their distinctive inflorescences and leaves. Further classification details, such as the specific rank and relationships within the family, are not well-documented for this species.
uses: I can find information on the medicinal uses of Enicostema occidentale. Enicostema occidentale, a plant native to Africa, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. It has been employed to treat fever, malaria, and other infectious diseases, as well as to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The plant's extracts have also shown anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Further research is needed to fully understand and confirm the medicinal uses of Enicostema occidentale.

parts: Enicostema occidentale is a plant species known for its medicinal properties, primarily found in the tropical regions of the world. The medicinal parts of this plant include the leaves, roots, and sometimes the bark. These parts are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. preparations: Enicostema occidentale is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Herbal preparations of Enicostema occidentale are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful for treating skin conditions and infections. side_effects: Enicostema occidentale, also known as Wild Sida, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes, but its use may lead to adverse effects if not properly managed.
